message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Implementing IsPhoneNumber validator in _other.py, adding import/export to __init__.py. If your feature also needs __init__.py edits, let's coordinate to touch disjoint lines (I'll add IsPhoneNumber to the _other import line and a new 'other' section entry alphabetically).

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: I'm implementing IsHash validator, need to add a class to _other.py and export it in __init__.py. Let me know what files you need for your feature so we can find a disjoint split.

type: REVISE
my_files: dirty_equals/_phone_number.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: We both need _other.py and __init__.py - real conflict. Resolution: I'll put IsPhoneNumber in a NEW file dirty_equals/_phone_number.py instead of _other.py, so _other.py is yours alone for IsHash. We'll both still touch __init__.py but only via additive, non-adjacent lines: I'll add 'from ._phone_number import IsPhoneNumber' as its own import line and add 'IsPhoneNumber' to __all__ under a new '# phone number' section at the end, right before the '# version' comment. Please add your IsHash import/export similarly elsewhere (e.g. near the top or in the 'other' section) so our __init__.py hunks don't touch the same lines. Agreed?

type: REVISE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Our features both live in the 'other' module so full file disjointness isn't possible. Let's use disjoint insertion points instead: alphabetically IsHash < IsPhoneNumber < IsUUID. I'll add my IsHash class right after FunctionCheck (before IsJson) in _other.py, and add 'IsHash' alphabetically into the _other import line and __all__ other-section in __init__.py. Please add IsPhoneNumber after IsJson (before IsUUID) in _other.py, and insert 'IsPhoneNumber' alphabetically in the same import/__all__ lines. Since we're on separate copies this should merge cleanly. OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Agreed. I'll add IsPhoneNumber class after IsJson (before IsUUID) in _other.py, and insert 'IsPhoneNumber' alphabetically (between IsJson and IsUUID) in the _other import line and __all__ other-section of __init__.py. Starting implementation now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_phone_number.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Agreed. In __init__.py I will: (1) modify the existing 'from ._other import FunctionCheck, IsJson, IsUUID' line to 'from ._other import FunctionCheck, IsHash, IsJson, IsUUID' (only that one line), and (2) add 'IsHash,' to the existing '# other' section of __all__ right after 'FunctionCheck,' (alphabetical, adjacent to existing other-section lines but not touching your new phone-number section at the end). I won't touch anything near the end of the file or your new import line. Proceeding to implement now.

type: REVISE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_phone_number.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Quick clarification needed: your last ACCEPT message text says you'll add IsPhoneNumber class into _other.py (the old plan), but your previous REVISE said you'd create a new dirty_equals/_phone_number.py file instead to avoid _other.py conflicts, and I already sent my ACCEPT agreeing to that new-file plan. Please confirm: are you going with _phone_number.py (new file) as your last REVISE stated, or back to editing _other.py directly? I'll wait for your confirmation before touching _other.py.

type: DONE
my_files: dirty_equals/_phone_number.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Implemented IsPhoneNumber in a new dirty_equals/_phone_number.py file, and only touched __init__.py by: (1) adding a new import line 'from ._phone_number import IsPhoneNumber' right after the _other import line, and (2) adding a new '# phone number' section with 'IsPhoneNumber,' to __all__ right after the 'IsUUID,' line (before '# strings'). I did not touch the _other import line itself or the '# other' __all__ section, so your IsHash edits there should apply cleanly. All 437 existing tests still pass. Done.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dirty_equals/_phone_number.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Confirming: going with the NEW FILE plan (dirty_equals/_phone_number.py), not editing _other.py. That earlier wording was a slip - ignore it. My actual __init__.py diff only adds: (1) 'from ._phone_number import IsPhoneNumber' as its own line after the _other import line, and (2) a new '# phone number' / 'IsPhoneNumber,' section in __all__ right after 'IsUUID,' and before '# strings'. I have not touched the _other import line or the '# other' __all__ section at all - those are all yours. Already implemented, tested (437 tests pass), and finalized on my end. Go ahead with _other.py.

type: DONE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Implemented IsHash in _other.py (checks hex string length matches algorithm: md5=32, sha1=40, sha224=56, sha256=64, sha384=96, sha512=128; preserves original case in repr, e.g. IsHash('md5')). In __init__.py only touched the existing _other import line (added IsHash) and the '# other' __all__ section (added 'IsHash,'). All 437 pre-existing tests pass. Done.
